items.briefing:
Microsoft has released patches fixing a record 570 security flaws this month — if your work computer hasn't updated recently, now is the time. Separately, security researchers are warning that cybercriminals are now using AI to conduct attacks in real time, making threats faster and harder to detect. This is particularly relevant for Australian organisations, as AI-powered attacks can be more convincing and targeted than traditional methods.

**Practical tip:** Check that your Windows device has installed the latest updates — go to Settings > Windows Update and click "Check for updates."

featured.title: AI now drives live cyber attacks, Check Point says
featured.quote: AI-powered cyber attacks are faster and harder to trace, escalating threats for Australian organizations.
